title: What happens when air raid sirens go off in Israel
author: CBS News
fullText: Okay, it's day four of a war President Trump says could go on for weeks. In and around Iran, tensions only grow more and more intense. We can tell you six US service members lives have been lost in the Pentagon's Operation Epic Fury. We're also learning US forces hit one of Iran's underground nuclear facilities. CBS News senior national security correspondent Charlie Dagetta joins us live continuing days of live and exclusive coverage. Charlie, we understand just moments ago sirens went off. What happens in those moments and then paint a picture about why they're going off. >> Yeah, Reed. So, we were just about to go live and uh we heard the air raid sirens and what what normally happens is we get um we get messages or warnings on our phone, which is kind of like a pre-warning to get low and then when the air raid sirens come that means it's now localized. And I have to point out, by the way, you might get the impression that this is happening all the time. It's not, even though this is a relatively small country, it's not across all of Israel, which means that if it's going to happen where we're standing in Tel Aviv, they're not alerting people in places like Hifa or Elat or any of those places. That means that is a a clear and present danger, right? So, when that happened, we got to pay attention to those things. And there are, you know, shelters on almost every one of these floors or you're going to kind of have to get low. And it's something you have to take seriously because just a couple of blocks away from here an Iranian missile has landed less than a mile. Another one has because although Israel has been engaged with the conflict with Hamas which are mostly rockets in Iron Dome have been able very very effective at uh intercepting those incoming rockets. Missiles are a different thing. They come in fast. They come in high. They use David's sling in order to inter intercept them. Sometimes they spread out. Sometimes it can be drones, etc., etc. And now when you're asking about an update of where we are right now, it's not just Iran because Hezbollah has joined this as well. And Hezbollah, as we've seen in the past, and I've been here to see it, Hezbollah much closer, can launch these uh missiles and rockets. They come in faster. They don't travel the kind of distance where they could be picked up from some of the interceptors. And that we didn't get a text for today. That just came up. We heard the air raid sirens. And again, when that happens, you got to get out of the way really quickly. And it's it's not bad theater here, you know, like we'll stay if if it's not a threat, but when it is a threat um and it's close, we have to get low. >> You like in terms of people that you asked me about earlier. >> Well, yes. And not that's a very good point. like yesterday or two days ago, we went to um a missile strike area that had been hit here in Tel Aviv. And there are other places where actual shelters have been hit. And even if you're in a shelter, if you take a direct hit, that doesn't necessarily mean that you're going to be safe. Nine people were killed inside a shelter underneath or near a synagogue or in a synagogue compound. When these things come in, they they take out everything in a huge radius. So, yes, we're talking about tens of thousands, hundreds of thousands of people who are spending like the night in in in shelters because you're interrupted so often that you're just better off just staying down there rather than having to go down every hour or two. So, that's the kind of situation we're in. But, you were talking about what's happening now um with operation operation epic fury. We just got an update from Sentcom. You'll forgive me if I look down on the notes. US assets employed. So, we know B1 bombers, B2 bombers have already been engaged. Uh some of those striking uh some of Iran's nuclear sites or suspected nuclear sites, B-52s have been involved. Something that I had reported months ago. There's there's what's called a Lucas system, and that's where the United States and Central Command have been launching one-way drones. We expect that from from Iran, and as a matter of fact, they kind of reverse engineered some of the Iran uh technology. they picked up off the the battlefields of Ukraine. But now, Sentcom, Central Command, who's in charge of this area of responsibility, has been launching these one-way drones, these Lucas drone systems, Patriots, as you know, these are interceptors, the THADs, F-15 fighter jets, F-16 fighter jets, F-18, F-22, F-35. I could go on and on and on, but these are all of the the the assets that have been involved um in Operation Epic Fury, and they continue to be involved. And I reported yesterday that the USS Gerald Rar Ford, remember we've been talking about this for weeks about how long it's taking to get to this region. It's been turned around from Southcom and now it's here. So I have confirmed that the Ford in addition to the USS Abraham Lincoln are both actively engaged in the conflict against Iran. >> Well, Charlie, we wish you great safety, you and your crew, and express great gratitude that you're there as a touchstone to bringing these stories to us. Please be safe. Thank you so much, brother. We appreciate it. Journalism matters, especially in >> I appreciate that. Thank you. Okay.
